title: Classic carrot cake
subtitle: Two layers of spiced carrot sponge with cream cheese frosting
excerpt: A two-layer oil-based carrot cake with walnuts and warm spice, sandwiched and coated with cream cheese frosting. The oil keeps the crumb moist for several days.
sourceNotes: The cream cheese-frosted carrot cake entered British baking consciousness largely through American influence in the 1970s and 1980s, appearing in early health-food bakery menus as a supposedly virtuous cake. In both Mary Berry's Baking Bible (1994) and Nigella Lawson's How to Be a Domestic Goddess (2000), the recipe appears in near-identical form to the American original. The oil-based sponge and cream cheese frosting together constitute the fixed canon; walnuts and mixed spice are the only significant regional variations.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the unfrosted layers individually, wrapped in cling film, for up to 2 months. Frost and assemble after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The assembled cake keeps well in the fridge for up to 3 days. Bring to room temperature for 1 hour before serving.

baking.decoratingTechnique: cream cheese frosting applied between layers and over the top and sides; walnut halves to finish

content.text: A carrot cake made with oil rather than butter stays moister longer, which is why it can last 3 days in the fridge without drying out. Grate the carrot on the coarse side of the box grater. Fine gratings disappear into the batter; coarse ones stay visible and give texture.

The cream cheese frosting must be made with full-fat cream cheese. Low-fat cream cheese contains more water and will not hold its shape; the frosting will be loose and may slide off the sides of the cake.

Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line two 20 cm round cake tins. Whisk the {{vegetable-oil}}, {{eggs}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} together in a large bowl. Stir in the {{light-brown-sugar}}. Fold in the {{plain-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{ginger-ground}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}}. Add the grated {{carrot}} and {{walnuts}} and fold until evenly distributed. Divide between the tins and bake for 30 to 35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack to cool completely.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} until smooth. Beat in the cold {{cream-cheese}} until just combined. Add the {{icing-sugar}} and {{vanilla-extract}} and beat briefly until smooth. Do not overbeat or the frosting will become too loose. Place one sponge layer on a plate or board. Spread one-third of the frosting over the top. Place the second layer on top and spread the remaining frosting over the top and sides. Press walnut halves from the remaining {{walnuts}} around the top edge. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.
